name: data-lineage-viz
description: Visualize object dependencies and data lineage from .dacpac files or by importing from SQL Server, Azure SQL, Fabric DW, and Synapse databases

commands.title: Data Lineage: Toggle Schema Overview Mode
Data Lineage: Show AI Trace in Graph

dataLineageViz.maxNodes.markdownDescription: Maximum number of database objects to load. The graph rendering is separately capped by `renderLimit`. Values above 1000 are supported for AI/data queries but the GUI will show schema overview or a limit warning.

dataLineageViz.renderLimit.markdownDescription: Maximum rendered nodes before showing a warning instead of the graph. Prevents browser performance issues with very large visualizations. AI tools and BFS operate on the full model regardless of this limit.

dataLineageViz.excludePatterns.markdownDescription: Regex patterns to permanently exclude objects by name at load time (e.g. `_TEST$`, `^staging\\.`). Matched against `schema.object`. Supports `%` wildcard. Requires reloading the data source to take effect. For interactive ad-hoc filtering without reload, use the Exclusion Rules toolbar button.

dataLineageViz.externalRefs.enabled.markdownDescription: Detect external references (OPENROWSET file paths, cross-database 3-part names). When disabled, no virtual external nodes are created. External Tables are unaffected.

dataLineageViz.overview.enabled.markdownDescription: Enable schema overview mode. When enabled and the graph exceeds `overview.threshold` nodes, the initial view shows schema-level bubbles instead of all objects. Set to `false` to always show the full object graph.

dataLineageViz.overview.threshold.markdownDescription: Node count above which schema overview mode auto-activates on initial load. Only applies when `overview.enabled` is `true`.

dataLineageViz.overview.forceOverviewThr.markdownDescription: Node count above which overview mode is forced even after manual toggle (soft guard). Prevents accidentally rendering very large graphs. Must be >= `overview.threshold`.

dataLineageViz.parseRulesFile.markdownDescription: Path to a custom parse rules YAML file. Leave empty for built-in defaults. [Customization guide](https://github.com/ChrisDevRepo/vscode_data_lineage/blob/main/docs/PARSE_RULES.md)

dataLineageViz.dmvQueryTimeout.markdownDescription: Timeout (seconds) per DMV query. Increase for large databases.

dataLineageViz.dmvQueriesFile.markdownDescription: Path to a custom DMV queries YAML file. Leave empty for built-in defaults. [Customization guide](https://github.com/ChrisDevRepo/vscode_data_lineage/blob/main/docs/DMV_QUERIES.md)

dataLineageViz.tableStatistics.enabled.markdownDescription: Show column statistics and row counts in the table design viewer (database import only).

dataLineageViz.tableStatistics.standardM.markdownDescription: Allow **Standard** profiling mode (adds AVG, STDEV, zero/empty counts). When `false`, only Quick mode is available — lighter queries.

dataLineageViz.tableStatistics.excludeEx.markdownDescription: Skip profiling for external tables. External tables query remote data sources (S3, Blob, other databases) — profiling can be slow and expensive.

dataLineageViz.tableStatistics.sampleThr.markdownDescription: Advanced: Tables above this row count use sampling instead of a full scan. Set to `0` to always sample.

dataLineageViz.tableStatistics.sampleSiz.markdownDescription: Advanced: Number of rows to sample when profiling large tables.

dataLineageViz.tableStatistics.useApprox.markdownDescription: Advanced: Use `APPROX_COUNT_DISTINCT` instead of exact `COUNT(DISTINCT)`. Much faster with ~2% margin. Requires SQL Server 2019+.

dataLineageViz.tableStatistics.maxColumn.markdownDescription: Advanced: Maximum number of columns to profile per table. Columns beyond this cap are skipped. Prevents massive queries on wide tables.

dataLineageViz.layout.direction.markdownDescription: Direction of the dependency graph layout.

enumDescriptions: Smooth step edges with rounded corners

dataLineageViz.layout.edgeAnimation.markdownDescription: Animate edges when running a trace.

dataLineageViz.layout.highlightAnimation.markdownDescription: Animate edges when clicking a node.

dataLineageViz.layout.minimapEnabled.markdownDescription: Show the graph minimap for navigation.

dataLineageViz.layout.rankSeparation.markdownDescription: Advanced: Horizontal spacing between dependency layers (pixels).

dataLineageViz.layout.nodeSeparation.markdownDescription: Advanced: Vertical spacing between nodes in the same layer (pixels).

dataLineageViz.trace.defaultUpstreamLeve.markdownDescription: How many levels upstream (inputs) to trace by default.

dataLineageViz.trace.defaultDownstreamLe.markdownDescription: How many levels downstream (outputs) to trace by default.

dataLineageViz.analysis.hubMinDegree.markdownDescription: Minimum number of connections for an object to appear in the hub list.

dataLineageViz.analysis.islandMaxSize.markdownDescription: Max connected component size to qualify as an island. Default (500) matches `maxNodes` — shows all disconnected subgraphs. Set lower (e.g. 5) to focus on small isolated pairs/groups only.

dataLineageViz.analysis.longestPathMinNo.markdownDescription: Minimum number of objects in a dependency chain for longest-path analysis.

dataLineageViz.ai.enabled.description: Enable the @lineage chat participant and AI lineage tools. Disable for users who do not use VS Code Copilot or want to remove AI features entirely.

dataLineageViz.ai.searchMaxResults.markdownDescription: Maximum results per `@lineage` object or DDL search. Auto-scales with the selected model's context window when not explicitly set. Raise for large projects with high-capacity models (Claude max tier, strong local LLMs).

dataLineageViz.ai.bfsMaxNodes.markdownDescription: Maximum nodes returned by `@lineage` BFS trace queries. Auto-scales with the selected model's context window when not explicitly set.

dataLineageViz.ai.bfsMaxEdges.markdownDescription: Maximum edges returned by `@lineage` BFS trace queries. Auto-scales with the selected model's context window when not explicitly set.

dataLineageViz.ai.analysisMaxGroups.markdownDescription: Maximum groups returned by `@lineage` analysis queries (hubs, islands, orphans, cycles). Auto-scales with the selected model's context window when not explicitly set.

dataLineageViz.ai.maxDdlChars.markdownDescription: Maximum DDL body characters per object (per single `getObjectDetail` call). When exceeded, an error is returned instead of partial DDL. Synapse/DWH stored procedures are typically 15,000–80,000 chars — raise to `500000` to effectively disable the limit. Auto-scales to `500000` when the selected model's context exceeds 128K tokens (e.g. Claude Sonnet).

chatParticipants.name: lineage
chatParticipants.description: Ask questions about your loaded lineage graph — tables, procedures, dependencies, data flow.

commands.name: trace
commands.description: Trace data lineage upstream or downstream from an object

commands.name: search
commands.description: Search for database objects by name or schema

commands.name: explain
commands.description: Explain what a stored procedure or view does

disambiguation.description: Questions about database dependencies, data flow, stored procedures, table lineage, SQL object relationships, where data comes from or goes to
examples: Trace the data flow for orders
What tables does this procedure read from
Show me the lineage for the financehub schema
What are the upstream sources of this table

languageModelTools.name: lineage_get_context
languageModelTools.userDescription: Shows what is currently loaded and visible in the lineage graph.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Call ONCE at start. Returns schemas (with per-type counts), stats, active filter, saved views. Use schema names in other tools' schemas[] filters.

languageModelTools.name: lineage_search_objects
languageModelTools.userDescription: Search for database objects by name.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Search by name (substring or regex). Returns IDs for BFS/detail. Use schemas[] to filter. For DDL body search use search_ddl. Never repeat same query.

types.description: Include only these object types. Omit for all.

schemas.description: Include only these schemas. Omit for all.

languageModelTools.name: lineage_get_object_detail
languageModelTools.userDescription: Get full details for a specific database object.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Full metadata for ONE object: columns, foreign keys, neighbors (up/dn), DDL. Use for single-object questions. For multi-object dependency analysis use run_bfs_trace.

languageModelTools.name: lineage_run_bfs_trace
languageModelTools.userDescription: Trace data lineage upstream and/or downstream from an object.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: PRIMARY lineage tool. Returns connected nodes with DDL (default). Read INSERT/SELECT in DDL to trace column-level data flow. Use schemas[] to limit scope on large models.

id.description: Exact object ID to trace from

types.description: Include only these node types. Omit for all.

schemas.description: Include only nodes in these schemas. Omit for all.

include_ddl.description: Include DDL/columns in results (default true). Set false for structure-only overview.

languageModelTools.name: lineage_run_analysis
languageModelTools.userDescription: Run structural analysis on the loaded lineage graph.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Structural graph analysis. Types: hubs (high-degree), islands (isolated subgraphs), orphans (no connections), longest-path, cycles, external-refs. Returns groups of node IDs with summary.

min_degree.description: Hubs only: minimum degree to qualify as hub. Default from config.

max_size.description: Islands only: maximum component size to include. Default from config.

languageModelTools.name: lineage_search_ddl
languageModelTools.userDescription: Search SQL body scripts for a text pattern.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Regex search across SP/view/function DDL bodies. Returns matching lines with context. Use for pattern-based DDL search; for simple keyword name search use search_objects instead.

query.description: Text or regex to search in DDL body scripts (max 200 chars)

types.description: Limit search to these object types. Default: all DDL-bearing types.

languageModelTools.name: lineage_create_ai_view
languageModelTools.userDescription: Create an AI-authored advanced bookmark with a curated set of nodes, narrative explanation, color groups, badges, and sticky notes. The user receives a VS Code notification and sees the view applied in the lineage graph.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Saves a named bookmark and applies it in the graph. Supports simple (name + node_ids) or rich views (narrative, highlight_groups, badges, notes). Annotation tiers (all optional): badge ≤15 chars (numbering/labels); note ≤200 chars (per-node logic/rules, \n for breaks); highlight_groups = color-code paths (bu/gn/rd/ye/or); narrative ≤500 chars (overall summary). Requires active project.

name.description: Display name for the view (max 60 chars)

node_ids.description: Exact node IDs to show (1–200, validated against catalog)

narrative.description: Plain text explanation of why these nodes matter (max 500 chars)

layout_direction.description: Graph layout direction — LR for sequential flows, TB for hierarchies (default TB)

node_ids.description: Node IDs in this group (must be subset of top-level node_ids)

badges.description: Short text chips shown on specific nodes (max 50)

notes.description: Sticky-note annotations — describe calcs, logic, or what each node does (max 50). Use \n for line breaks.

text.description: Note text (max 200 chars, \n for line breaks)

languageModelTools.name: lineage_get_ddl_batch
languageModelTools.userDescription: Get DDL body scripts for multiple objects in one call.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Batch DDL fetch for known object IDs (max 20). Use after run_bfs_trace with include_ddl:false. Returns DDL for scriptable objects, type-only for tables.

ids.description: Exact object IDs to fetch DDL for (max 20). Use IDs from lineage_run_bfs_trace or lineage_search_objects.

scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run build:ext && npm run build:webview
scripts.build: npm run build:ext && npm run build:webview
scripts.test: tsx test/dacpacExtractor.test.ts && tsx test/graphBuilder.test.ts && tsx test/parser-edge-cases.test.ts && tsx test/graphAnalysis.test.ts && tsx test/dmvExtractor.test.ts && tsx test/tsql-complex.test.ts && tsx test/projectStore.test.ts && tsx test/ai-tools.test.ts && tsx test/snapshot-aw-baseline.ts && vitest run --config vitest.config.ts
